content.text: Issued equipment, schedule A. Sign here. The signature is for the inventory, not the warranty.
A kit is who trained you — a starting bundle, not a progression. It costs nothing and it gates nothing.
A gear loadout. Weapons and armor by name — their stats live on Weapons and Armor.
The matching tree's entry augment, installed free. Its effect is printed on the tree's own page, where the rest of the tree waits.
One technique from the catalog. The catalog, its costs, and its training sources live on Techniques.
A recommended stat lean. Where the kit's training assumes your strengths sit. A recommendation, never a gate.

The free technique is a Trained-tier pick. Choose any Trained (300-class) technique from the catalog — the instructor requirement is waived; your kit's training included it. Higher rungs are bought in play — see Techniques.

Two contractors with the same kit are the same character for about one mission. The kit hands out the entry augment; the tree does the diverging — two Berserkers stop playing alike by their third augment (Trees Overview).
the kit is who the corp guessed you'd be. wrong, usually. takes a few missions to prove it.

kits.name: Berserker
kits.desc: Trained to get stronger as the fight bites — taking hits and taking kills both feed the engine.
kits.ability: One technique from the catalog — your pick (Techniques)

kits.name: Bulwark
kits.desc: Trained to hold ground — pins, entry punishment, and a door that hits back.
kits.ability: One technique from the catalog — your pick (Techniques)

kits.name: Channeler
kits.desc: Trained pattern-work — bank rounds before the fight, fire them clean during it.
kits.ability: One technique from the catalog — your pick (Techniques)

kits.name: Dancer
kits.desc: Trained to fight in motion — paired blades, and an Evasion that only exists while you move.
kits.ability: One technique from the catalog — your pick (Techniques)

kits.name: Leech
kits.desc: Trained to run hot — burn Charge into dice now, settle the bill after.
kits.ability: One technique from the catalog — your pick (Techniques)

kits.name: Handler
kits.desc: Trained beside a beast that takes its own turn — and trained as the voice that steers a squad's fight.
kits.ability: One technique from the catalog — your pick (Techniques)
kits.gear: Sidearm, trained Char Hound or Call-kit

kits.name: Operative
kits.desc: Trained for the Extreme-band theater — distance as home turf, and rounds that get counted for a reason.
kits.ability: One technique from the catalog — your pick (Techniques)

kits.name: Phantom
kits.desc: Trained for the approach — the Opener against anyone who hasn't acted yet.
kits.ability: One technique from the catalog — your pick (Techniques)

kits.name: Sin Eater
kits.desc: Trained to take what's hurting the squad and carry it as Sins.
kits.ability: One technique from the catalog — your pick (Techniques)

kits.name: Technician
kits.desc: Trained preparation — tuned items in prep slots, medicine for both health bars, a drone on your AP.
kits.ability: One technique from the catalog — your pick (Techniques)

content.text: Custom: 1,200 cr of any legal loadout, plus any one tree's entry augment. No kit name, no preset gear — you build the day-one package yourself.

Custom keeps the symmetry. A Custom kit grants the same package as the named ten: your chosen gear within the budget, one free tree entry augment, and one free Trained technique. Whatever budget you leave unspent walks with you as starting credits — see Finishing.
Kit issuance is complimentary. Equipment grade reflects projected first-quarter survival. Congratulations on your projection.
they told you exactly what they think you're worth. it's in writing. keep the receipt.
